_source: Authored 2026-07-19 (K119) from an offline CFG census of every slot-5 SCD routine (scratchpad census over decode_script.py disasm; pristine .dinorand-bak-preferred rooms). For each op-0x30 DOOR / op-0x31 ITEM AOT commit, records the op-0x07 conditional-skip spans enclosing it and the predicate feeding each span. This is the control-flow axis door-gating.json deliberately flattens (K107 noted the omission): a commit inside a skippable span means the door/pickup AOT is NEVER BUILT unless the predicate holds — distinct from lock_index locking (locked doors exist and show a message; guarded commits don't exist).
_semantics: Byte-proven on Dino2.exe.bak (offline capstone disx): op-0x07 handler 0x4876F0 — mask=1<<byte[op+1]; if [entity+0x9c]&mask (or byte[op+1]==0) then IP=op_addr+sext16(word[op+2]) (span SKIPPED), else IP+=4 (span EXECUTES). arg==0 is therefore an unconditional forward jump (else-branch marker). Cond-flag writer 0x48BC90: result==0 -> [0x9c]=0x2A (bit1 SET); result>0 -> 0x4C; result<0 -> 0x70 (bit1 CLEAR). op-0x03 (0x4875A0) and op-0x01 (0x487520) both finish with 0x48BC90(result), so the last value-producing op before the 0x07 decides. op-0x1d GetFlag leaves var[0]=1/0; the canonical tail [op03 ff-stackmove; push var0; op03 ff-stackmove; op01 var0:=stack] re-materializes var[0] through 0x48BC90 => arg=1 span EXECUTES IFF FLAG SET (polarity 'iff_set'). One op-0x03 operator=19 (LNOT, the ST105 spawn-once latch pattern) inverts => 'iff_clear'. Tails with any other value op are 'unknown' — no polarity is asserted for them. UPDATE (K121, same day): guard predicates are now SYMBOLICALLY RESOLVED (scratchpad stack/var evaluator over the decoded op semantics; 91/91 commits, 0 unresolved). Full ALU operator table 0x48BC20 (all 26 handlers disassembled): 0 ADD 1 SUB 2 MUL 3 DIV 4 MOD 5 OR 6 AND 7 XOR 8 NOT 9/11 SHL 10/12 SAR 13 GT 14 LT 15 GE 16 LE 17 EQ 18 NE 19 LNOT 20 INC 21 DEC 22/23 MOV 24 LOR 25 LAND. Each guard row's `expr` uses: flag(g,i) = GetFlag; CHARACTER(+0x108F) 0=Regina 1=Dylan; DIFFICULTY(+0x108E); vmEntryState = op-0x20; word(scene+0x10A8) = family-5 prop 0x1e (VERIFIED prev-room id word; writer 0x41EA98/0x41EABB). arg=1 rows execute iff expr != 0; arg=0 rows are the else-branch of the innermost preceding test.

_held_item_door_gates.mechanism: flag(9,0x2e) ownership test guards the door AOT commit
_held_item_door_gates.citation: ST10D blob: GetFlag(9,0x2e) test @0xe458 (pushes 05 00 09 00 / 05 00 2e 00 @0xe450/0xe454); op-0x07 arg=1 @0xe470 skip 0x5c spans 0xe474..0xe4cc; the ST10D->ST10F door commit @0xe4c0 (door-graph slot 2, dest push @0xe4b2 = 0x010f) lies inside; polarity iff_set per _semantics
_held_item_door_gates.itemSource: flag(9,0x2e) granted in ST10F routines 2/3 (items.json keyItemGrantSites, K101)
_held_item_door_gates.note: The ONLY cross-room held-key-item door gate in the corpus. Partially amends K107: the requiresItems axis is not empty — it has exactly this one offline-provable member.

_flag8_commit_guarded_doors: 106->10E & 10E->106 (flag(8,3), set by ST10E routine 2 script), 603->604 (flag(8,9), ST603 routine 4), 902->903 (flag(8,27), ST902 routine 1): door-EXISTENCE guards on group-8 flags, invisible to the lock_index model (none is in door-gating locked_doors). All three flags are script_set in the door's own room/pair => same-room event/puzzle gates (K107 producer model: vacuous as cross-room requiresRooms), but a door randomizer or tracker must know these doors are absent until the flag fires.

guards.cond: executes iff (((((vmEntryState == code0(+0x1204)) && (vmEntryState == code1(+0x1205))) && (vmEntryState == code2(+0x1206))) && (vmEntryState == code3(+0x1207)))) != 0

_subweapon_key_use_gates._mechanism: The 14 op-0x3a SAT-10 key_use records (door-gating.json) are DC2's vine/electric subweapon doors. Consumer 0x48B790 (byte-cited disasm 2026-07-19): reads the EQUIPPED SUB-WEAPON id byte scene+0x10B5 (equip word scene+0x10B4: lo=main, hi=sub; PSX mirror +0xc50/51 K80); sub id in {2,8} -> requires record subtype==1 (cmp byte[rec+0x14],1 @0x48B7C4); sub id in {1,7} -> requires subtype==2 (cmp @0x48B824); match -> actor poke + 0x48C220 + SetFlag(8, rec+0x15, 1) + record deactivate. Equipped-sub id encoding = catalog id - 0x10, witnessed twice: Dylan bootstrap equip word 0x0101 (main 0x01 Shotgun, sub 1 = 0x11 Machete) and Regina stored word 0x0202 @0x450E96 (main 0x02 Handgun, sub 2 = 0x12 Large Stungun); congruent with the K76 improve transforms 0x11->0x17 / 0x12->0x18 (= sub 1->7 / 2->8, both accepted). Catalog owner flags (weapon-catalog.json, PSX-verified): 0x11/0x17 owner Dylan, 0x12/0x18 owner Regina — so these are equip gates that are character gates in practice.

locked_door_requirements.requires: none — script_set (ST302 r5 / ST303 r6 story routines)

_character_door_gates._mechanism: Byte-proven 2026-07-19: op-0x12 binds syscall family (stack[1]) + arg selector; op-0x1f calls [0x7319D4 + family*4]. Family 5 = 0x488D30, a scene-property switch (arg-0x1e jump table 0x488F90); arg 0x22 (34) -> case 4 handler 0x488D8A returns byte[scene+0x108F] = CURRENT CHARACTER (0=Regina, 1=Dylan, 2=David... DC2-EXTRA-CRISIS-ROSTER-DECODE.md). Result compared via op-0x03 operator 17 = EQ (handler 0x48BBB8, sete) against a literal; canonical tail -> arg-1 condskip executes iff EQUAL. (ALU operator 19 = LNOT, 0x48BC01 — confirms the K119 polarity table.)

gates.cite: op12 args (5,0) @~0x12618; push 34 @0x12628; op1f @0x1262c; op03 EQ rval=1 @0x1263e; condskip @0x12654
push 34 @0x12390; op1f @0x12394; op03 EQ rval=1 @0x123a6; condskip @0x123bc
push 34 @0xe49c; op1f @0xe4a0; op03 EQ rval=0 @0xe4b2; condskip @0xe4c8 (plus flag(6,52) and flag(5,2) guards on the same commit)

_character_door_gates._note: Both 408/409 branches commit doors to the same dests — the character split selects door VARIANTS (flags/pose), not reachability. 607->700 is the one door committed for Regina only. Other syscall-guarded commits in door_guards/item_guards keep kind=syscall_0x1f unresolved unless annotated here.

_scene_properties_family5._source: op-0x1f family 5 = 0x488D30 (syscall table 0x7319D4[5]) — scene-property getter, arg-0x1e jump table 0x488F90, all 43 case handlers disassembled 2026-07-19. Getters only (no writes).

properties.0x1e: word: byte[scene+0x10A9]<<8 | byte[scene+0x10A8] (0xSRR room-id form; VERIFIED = PREVIOUS-ROOM id: transition writer 0x41EA98/0x41EABB copies old scene+0x1090/91 (current room lo/stage hi) into +0x10A8/A9 before overwriting)
properties.0x22: byte scene+0x108F = CURRENT CHARACTER (0=Regina,1=Dylan,2=David; EC roster)
properties.0x3c: s8 scene+0x108E = DIFFICULTY (0 Easy/1 Normal/2 Hard)

_resolved_notables.st102_pickup_K102_predicate: ST102 op-0x31 @0xB190 (K102's open 'predicate==difficulty?') RESOLVED: executes iff !flag(6,2) && CHARACTER==1 (Dylan). The op-0x1f K102 saw is the family-5 CHARACTER getter, NOT a difficulty query — the difficulty property (0x3c) is unused here. K102's live Hard-absent witness therefore needs reinterpretation (difficulty must act via flag(6,2)/character state, or the difficulty-capacity path only); named live witness to close: read flag(6,2) + char byte on a Hard ST102 entry.
_resolved_notables.st800_to_005_door: 800->005 (prior 'unknown polarity') RESOLVED: executes iff word(scene+0x10A8)==0x706 && flag(5,17) CLEAR; else-branch commits the @0x18516-sibling variant. 0x706 = ST706 id form. ST800 pickups @0x18748/@0x18858 are CHARACTER==0 (Regina)-gated.
_resolved_notables.vm_entry_state_doors: All four 'vm_entry_state' door guards (605->606, 606->605, 706->800, 800->706) resolve to !(vmEntryState) — committed only on entry-state 0 (normal entry), resume/cutscene entries skip them.
_resolved_notables.st10f_pickup_committed_doors: BOTH ST10F exit doors are interaction-committed: the Gas Mask SAT-1 pickup routines r2@0x60cc / r3@0x6156 each do SetFlag(9,0x2e,1) (the K101 grant) and then commit one exit door AOT (r2: @0x6140 ->10D with dest push 269=0x10D; r3: @0x61ca ->40B). The grant and the door creation are the same program — a door-rando or tracker must treat ST10F's exits as pickup-dependent, and the 10D->10F Gas Mask gate (K119) pairs with this on the far side.

_door_commit_provenance._source: K122 (2026-07-20): routine-reachability fixpoint over the complete slot-5 invocation graph — op-0x08 gosub (imm routine idx; handler 0x487730 target=[dir+idx*4]), op-0x15 start / op-0x19 restart (routine idx = stack[0]; ALL 303 sites resolve to literals once resolver 0x48BA50 is modeled correctly: modes other than 1/2/6 fall through to 0x48BA93 = literal, so the ubiquitous mode-4 pushes are literals), and AOT handler registrations (op-0x31 param / op-0x39 routine). Directory entry 0 = room-entry init. 667 routines: 186 proven start-on-entry (UNCOND), 481 conditional (guarded start, conditional caller, or player-interaction handler), 11 with NO discoverable start site at all (K123 closure below). op-0x22 REQUEST ROUTINE SWITCH (K65) is modeled as an invocation edge (vm_switch), attributing the endgame/event door chains (903->904, 904->003, 407->408, 800->005/801, 808->809, ...).

_classes.on-entry: committed by an unconditionally-running routine, no guards — exists on plain room entry
_classes.on-entry-guarded: in an entry-run routine but inside op-0x07 guard(s) — see guards[]
_classes.event-committed: in a routine only started by conditional script flow (cutscene/story) — see routine_how
_classes.interaction-committed: in an op-0x31 pickup / op-0x39 examine HANDLER — exists only after the player interaction fires it
_classes.no-start-site-found: containing routine has NO discoverable start site (see _invocation_model_closure); cutscene-native or dead code — availability unproven offline

_invocation_model_closure.native_start_surface: COMPLETE .text xref census: exactly 5 callers of the routine starter 0x48BF20 — (1) 0x487A33 in the op-0x15 handler; (2) 0x48B5DD in the SAT-1 pickup handler (entity=byte[rec+0x14], routine=word[rec+0x18]); (3) 0x48B765 in the SAT-9 examine/use handler (routine=word[rec+0x14], entity=byte[rec+0x18], K118); (4) 0x48BD4E room-load bootstrap: zeroes the 10-entity VM array then 0x48BF20(0,0) = starts directory routine 0 (proves dir-0 = init); (5) 0x48BF06: 0x48BF20(0, byte[0x878C07]) — one-shot consumer of the op-0x22 REQUEST ROUTINE SWITCH byte (only writers: op-0x22 handler 0x48917C, the VM-reset 0xFF clears 0x48BD13/0x48BF0E) — SAME-room switch, not cross-room.
_invocation_model_closure.tail_audit: The 85,000 'unwalked' span bytes (12.3% of the corpus, first-RETURN walker limit) were recursively descended via every op-0x07 skip target: only 1,764 bytes are reachable code, containing ZERO door/item/examine/key_use/start ops — the tails are data (op-0x11 glyph text etc.). AUDIT PASSED: door-graph.json / item-placements.json miss nothing; the first-RETURN caveat is immaterial for records.
_invocation_model_closure.fallthrough: All 11 no-start-site routines' predecessors end in a clean RETURN exactly at the routine boundary — fallthrough execution refuted for every one.
_invocation_model_closure.ce_witness: One exec-BP on 0x48BF20 logging (entity, routine) while traversing these rooms (esp. 408<->409 as both characters) decides cutscene-native vs dead for each.
